seo.meta_title: Auth.js vs WorkOS for Next.js App Router (2026): Which Auth Tool Wins? (Updated Week 24, 2026)
seo.meta_description: Stop guessing between Auth.js and WorkOS. Here's a practical comparison of how both handle Next.js authentication  –  pricing, developer experience, and where each one falls short.
seo.og_title: Auth.js vs WorkOS — I Tested Both So You Don't Have To
seo.og_description: I tested Auth.js and WorkOS with Next.js App Router. Here's my honest comparison of pricing, DX, and which one I'd actually ship.

hero.headline: Auth.js vs WorkOS for Next.js Authentication
hero.subheadline: An honest comparison of Auth.js and WorkOS — pricing, DX, setup time, and which one we recommend for Next.js App Router projects in 2026.

quick_summary: Here's the honest take: both Auth.js and WorkOS will get the job done. But Auth.js makes it noticeably easier. The 8/10 vs 5/10 gap in Next.js scores tells the story. WorkOS makes sense if you are optimizing for No free tier (enterprise sales). Otherwise, Auth.js wins.
verdict_text: For this matchup, Auth.js comes out ahead. Look, the Next.js integration (8/10) is genuinely better in practice  –  not just on paper. Honestly, workOS is a good tool with its own strengths, particularly around no free tier (enterprise sales). Choose accordingly.

pros: Massive community with extensive documentation and examples
Framework-agnostic but excellent Next.js support via next-auth
Supports 80+ OAuth providers out of the box
Battle-tested in production across thousands of applications
cons: No built-in UI components - you build your own login pages
Configuration can become complex for advanced use cases
Database setup required for persistent sessions

pros: Enterprise-grade SSO with SAML/OIDC support out of the box
SCIM provisioning for enterprise directory integration
SOC 2 compliant and enterprise-ready from day one
Clean API design with excellent documentation
Used by Vercel, Airbnb, and other top-tier tech companies
cons: No free tier - requires sales conversation to get started
Not designed for consumer auth or B2C applications
Pricing is opaque and requires a sales call
Overkill for teams that don't need enterprise SSO

faq.question: Should I choose Auth.js or WorkOS for my Next.js app?
faq.answer: It depends on your specific needs. Auth.js scores 8/10 for Next.js integration with a Medium learning curve. WorkOS scores 5/10 with a Advanced learning curve. For most projects, we recommend Auth.js because of its stronger DX and better alignment with App Router patterns. However, if Yes, fully open source or No free tier (enterprise sales) is a deciding factor, the cheaper option may serve you well.

faq.question: Is Auth.js or WorkOS more affordable?
faq.answer: Auth.js starts at Free (Open Source) with a free tier of Yes, fully open source. WorkOS starts at Custom pricing (enterprise) with a free tier of No free tier (enterprise sales). The total cost of ownership depends on your MAU (monthly active users). At lower volumes, the free tiers of both are sufficient. As you scale, Auth.js offers better value at scale. Factor in hidden costs like enterprise features, SSO, and database infrastructure when comparing.

faq.question: Is Auth.js or WorkOS easier to set up with Next.js?
faq.answer: Auth.js has a Medium learning curve with an estimated setup time of 20 minutes. WorkOS has a Advanced learning curve with an estimated setup time of 45 minutes. Auth.js is faster to set up, but ease of integration also depends on your team's familiarity with the framework and whether you want pre-built UI components or prefer to build your own.

faq.question: Can I use Auth.js and WorkOS together?
faq.answer: Using both Auth.js and WorkOS in the same Next.js application is not recommended. They would create separate session management systems, conflicting middleware, and competing cookies. Choose one auth provider and stick with it. Migrating between auth providers later is possible but requires user data export and rebuilding authentication flows.

faq.question: Which has better developer experience: Auth.js or WorkOS?
faq.answer: Auth.js has a Medium learning curve, a Next.js integration score of 8/10, and a user rating of 4.6★. WorkOS has a Advanced learning curve, 5/10, and 4★. On paper, Auth.js leads in DX. In practice, your specific requirements around pre-built components, customization, and team expertise matter just as much. Try both free tiers to see what clicks.
